name: Wynton Kelly
abstract: Wynton Charles Kelly (December 2, 1931 – April 12, 1971) was an American jazz pianist and composer. He is known for his lively, blues-based playing and as one of the finest accompanists in jazz.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert
Miles Davis with Gil Evans and Orchestra

works.t: Call It Stormy Monday (But Tuesday Is Just as Bad)

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Evans
about_quotes.evidence: Wynton Kelly is mentioned by the interviewer as an example of a pianist who replaced Red Garland in Bill Evans' trio; the interviewee does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with Wynton Kelly.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Evans interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 22, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a recording that I did with Wynton Kelly, Jimmy Cobb and Hank Mobley... I was swinging with Wynton Kelly and Jimmy Cobb

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: On that date we had Paul Chambers, Wynton Kelly, Charlie Persip, Gigi Gryce, Art Farmer, Shahib Shihab.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: The piano player was Wynton Kelly. So, when she sang – she was only traveling with a piano player. The piano player was Wynton Kelly. So, when she sang, it was her, Wynton, Keter Betts, and myself played for her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Because I joined Dizzy’s band... And the band that I joined was the band with Lee Morgan, Benny Golson, Wynton Kelly...
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: And in '61 you made one with Wynton Kelly and Teddy Edwards, do you remember that? ... Yeah, that was a great session.
